nodes.label: Database Snapshot Encryption
nodes.rationale: The Postgres dump is encrypted with age before leaving the runner, and the plaintext dump is removed.

nodes.label: Supabase Media Replication
nodes.rationale: Private media is copied from Supabase Storage to a separately configured R2 destination.

nodes.label: Immutable R2 Archive
nodes.rationale: Dated backup objects are copied with immutable and checksum safeguards to prevent accidental overwrite.

nodes.label: Backup Integrity Verification
nodes.rationale: Checksums, an object manifest, rclone check, and a final encrypted-dump size check provide evidence that the snapshot exists and matches its source.

nodes.label: Query-First Graph Navigation
nodes.rationale: Scoped query, path, and explain results are preferred because they are usually much smaller than the full report or raw search output.

nodes.label: Incremental Graph Maintenance
nodes.rationale: Running graphify update after code changes keeps the project graph current without API cost.

nodes.label: Atomic Idea-to-Goal Promotion
nodes.rationale: Promotion creates one shared goal, reparents its content, and removes the original idea as one database transaction.

nodes.label: Goalpost Privacy Model
nodes.rationale: Private planning state is separated sharply from public, indexable goal content; blocking limits interaction but cannot hide open-web content.

nodes.label: Reproducible Local Toolchain
nodes.rationale: mise pins Node.js and pnpm consistently for developers and CI while setup generates local Supabase configuration without printing credentials.

nodes.label: Production Configuration
nodes.rationale: Staging and production use separate Supabase and Vercel environments, with migrations validated in staging before production.

nodes.label: Deferred Beta Scope
nodes.rationale: Native clients, private goals, feeds, messaging, AI features, and other listed capabilities are intentionally outside the beta.

nodes.label: Hybrid Rendering Boundary
nodes.rationale: Public routes render on the server for canonical metadata and crawlable HTML, while interactive editing and realtime listeners remain client components.

nodes.label: Database-Enforced Authorization
nodes.rationale: Supabase is the authorization source of truth, so Server Actions do not replace RLS and high-integrity functions validate auth.uid().

nodes.label: Service-Role Boundary
nodes.rationale: The service-role key is restricted to server-side deletion, notification fan-out, moderation, and scheduled reminder processing.

nodes.label: Safe Rich-Text Rendering
nodes.rationale: Known TipTap and ProseMirror nodes are rendered directly instead of injecting HTML, and links are limited to HTTP(S).

nodes.label: Atomic Promotion Transaction
nodes.rationale: The transaction locks and validates the source idea, creates the goal and collaborators, reparents content, records activity, and rolls back entirely on failure.

nodes.label: Authorized Realtime Broadcast
nodes.rationale: Topic-specific policies admit only the target user, list members, or goal collaborators before the app refreshes server-rendered state.

nodes.label: Idempotent Reminder Delivery
nodes.rationale: Row locking, unique notification source keys, Resend idempotency keys, and bounded retries prevent duplicate delivery.

nodes.label: Upload Safety Pipeline
nodes.rationale: Uploads are validated by type, size, signatures, Office structure, and macro markers; dangerous formats are rejected and private URLs require Storage RLS authorization.

nodes.label: Staged Deployment Order
nodes.rationale: Migrations, generated types, reminder delivery, and browser smoke checks are validated in staging before the same migration commit and application are deployed to production.

nodes.label: Forward-Only Migrations
nodes.rationale: An applied migration is preserved and corrected with a new migration rather than rewritten.

nodes.label: Encrypted Backup Lifecycle
nodes.rationale: Daily encrypted database and media snapshots are retained in private R2 for 30 days while the age private key remains outside GitHub and R2.

nodes.label: Monthly Restore Rehearsal
nodes.rationale: Monthly restoration into a disposable project verifies that database and media backups are recoverable before an incident.

nodes.label: Account Deletion and Backup Retention
nodes.rationale: Live data is removed immediately while encrypted snapshots retain prior state only until their documented 30-day expiry.

nodes.label: Incident Containment
nodes.rationale: Suspension, report resolution, and unsafe-upload handling are separate containment actions because no single action hides every form of public content.

nodes.label: Secret Isolation
nodes.rationale: Provider credentials rotate independently, and server secrets must never be exposed through NEXT_PUBLIC variables.

nodes.label: Reproducible Local Environment
nodes.rationale: Pinned tools, frozen dependencies, local containers, migrations, and a generated ignored environment file create a repeatable development stack without printing secrets.

nodes.label: Authenticated End-to-End Journey
nodes.rationale: The journey uses a disposable user to verify onboarding, idea capture, public-goal promotion, and cleanup, and must never target production.

nodes.label: Local Reminder Test
nodes.rationale: The hosted schedule is absent locally, so the reminder Edge Function is served and invoked manually with an ignored local secret file.

nodes.label: Staging-First Release Validation
nodes.rationale: Migrations, authentication, reminders, uploads, backups, and the full smoke journey are validated in staging before production.

nodes.label: Nightly Backup Configuration
nodes.rationale: Production backup readiness requires scoped GitHub environment secrets, a private R2 bucket, an age public key, a manual workflow run, and a verified disposable restore.

nodes.label: Production Release Gate
nodes.rationale: Release requires passing CI, staging acceptance, matching production migration and application revisions, production feature verification, monitoring, and a recent encrypted backup.
